preview_text:
South Korea enters as the model favorite with a 62% win probability, leaving South Africa at 13% and a draw at 25%. The gap between the two sides reflects a meaningful disparity in the model's assessment, though South Africa is not without a path to victory.

The draw probability of 25% suggests this match carries competitive uncertainty despite the favorite's advantage. South Korea's win probability exceeds 60%, but the substantial draw chance indicates the model does not view the outcome as heavily skewed toward regulation time resolution. South Africa's 13% win probability reflects underdog status without suggesting futility.

The model and sportsbook consensus are broadly aligned. South Korea sits within 3 percentage points across all three outcomes, and Polymarket prices track similarly. The model leans marginally lower on South Africa (13% versus 17% in the book consensus) and marginally higher on South Korea (62% versus 59%), but these gaps are well within normal variance. No material disagreement exists between sources on the likely structure of this fixture.
